input.content: What is the final speed of an object dropped from rest after falling for 5 seconds if we neglect air resistance?

tools.name: calculate_final_speed
tools.description: Calculate the final speed of an object in free fall after a certain time, neglecting air resistance. The acceleration due to gravity is considered as -9.81 m/s^2 Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

initial_speed.description: The initial speed of the object in m/s. Default is 0 for an object at rest.

time.description: The time in seconds for which the object is in free fall.

gravity.description: The acceleration due to gravity. Default is -9.81 m/s^2. This is a float type value.

messages.content:

- Do not ask for values that are already explicit in prior user turns, tool outputs, or the current working state.
- Before using path-sensitive tools, ground the current working directory and existing file or directory names from prior tool outputs instead of assuming the root directory.
- When emitting tool calls, keep assistant content empty and avoid adding a free-form status summary in the same message.
